title: Minimum Window Substring
prompt: Given strings `s` and `t`, return the minimum window substring of `s` that contains every character of `t` (including duplicates). If no such substring exists, return the empty string. Any valid minimum-length window is accepted.
constraints: s and t consist of uppercase and lowercase English letters.
hints: Slide a window and maintain how many of `t`'s characters are still required.
Expand right; once the window contains all chars, shrink left as much as possible.
Track best window seen; reset it whenever you find a smaller valid window.

optimal.approach: Sliding window with a `need`/`have` count and a `missing` counter.

pitfalls: Decrementing missing when the same char appears beyond what t requires.
